description: §38.1 module-file dispensation (S87 Insight 30) — a `<channel>` at file top level in a PURE-CHANNEL-FILE (a file containing NO `<program>` element anywhere) is canonical placement and does NOT fire E-CHANNEL-OUTSIDE-PROGRAM. This proves the `fileHasProgram` guard is load-bearing: file-top is a violation ONLY when a `<program>` sibling exists (contrast `channel/outside-program`).
rationale: §38.1 dispensation: a `<channel>` at file top in a file with no `<program>` element is the PURE-CHANNEL-FILE shape (§38.12.6), canonical per the engine-parity precedent (§21.8/B14) and SILENT on E-CHANNEL-OUTSIDE-PROGRAM.
